perturbation.original_text: Each Party, for itself and its associates as defined below, represents and warrants that it shall not conduct business with any sources or contacts, or said source's or contact's associates as defined below, that are originally made known and/or available by another Party hereto, at any time or in any manner, without the express written permission (not to be unreasonably withheld) of the Party who made the source(s) known and/or available.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party, for itself and its affiliates as defined below, represents and warrants that it shall not conduct business with any sources or contacts, or said source's or contact's associates as defined below, that are originally made known and/or available by another Party hereto, at any time or in any manner, without the express written permission (not to be unreasonably withheld) of the Party who made the source(s) known and/or available.
perturbation.explanation: The term "associates" is changed to "affiliates". Associates is defined later in the contract, and now it is not clear if "affiliates" falls under that definition. Is it referring to defined associates, or undefined affiliates
perturbation.contradicted_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the term "associates" or "contacts" shall be defined as: in the case of a business entity its officers, directors, affiliates, subsidiaries, associated entities, and any other business entity in which the business entity owns five percent (5%) or more of the outstanding equity interest.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'associates' with 'affiliates'. The contradicted text defines 'associates', which includes affiliates, subsidiaries, associated entities etc. By changing the original text to 'affiliates', it is now unclear if the term refers to the affiliates as defined within the definition of "associates", causing confusion and a potential contraction because it is not referring to any defined term, so a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties will maintain complete confidentiality regarding this Agreement and all transactions occurring thereunder, each other's business, business sources and affiliates and each other's propriety knowledge and know-how, and will disclose such information only pursuant to the express written permission of the party who made such information available save where such information deemed to be in the public domain or under the order of a competent Court or Government Agency.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties will maintain complete confidentiality regarding this Agreement and all transactions occurring thereunder, each other's business, business sources and holdings and each other's propriety knowledge and know-how, and will disclose such information only pursuant to the express written permission of the party who made such information available save where such information deemed to be in the public domain or under the order of a competent Court or Government Agency.
perturbation.explanation: The term "affiliates" is changed to "holdings". It is unclear if holding falls into affiliates as defined elsewhere.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For purposes of this Agreement, the term "associates" or "contacts" shall be defined as: in the case of a business entity its officers, directors, affiliates, subsidiaries, associated entities, and any other business entity in which the business entity owns five percent (5%) or more of the outstanding equity interest.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'affiliates', which is then changed to 'holdings'. The contradicted text defines 'affiliates' within the definition of 'associates' or 'contacts'. By changing 'affiliates' to 'holdings', it's unclear if 'holdings' falls under the definition of 'affiliates' (as it appears in the contradicted text's definition), thus creating a misalignment in terminology that may change the scope of who or what is considered an 'associate' or 'contact'. This ambiguity directly contradicts the specific definition provided, leading to a potential contradiction as described by the explanation and the Misaligned Terminology definition.

perturbation.original_text: It is understood and agreed that by reason of this "Agreement" the "Parties" that are involved during the course of business transactions may learn from one another, or from the principals the names, addresses, telephone numbers <PAGE> of lenders, agents, brokers, clients or others hereafter referred to as "Contracts" and or "Associates".
perturbation.changed_text: It is understood and agreed that by reason of this "Agreement" the "Parties" that are involved during the course of business transactions may learn from one another, or from the principals the names, addresses, telephone numbers <PAGE> of lenders, agents, brokers, clients or others hereafter referred to as "Deals" and or "Associates".
perturbation.explanation: The term "Contracts" is changed to "Deals". This conflicts with definition of contract later on.
perturbation.contradicted_text: It is understood and agreed that the "Contracts" of each party hereto are and shall be recognized as exclusive and valuable "Contracts" and that the parties will not directly or indirectly negotiate or participate in any transaction circumventing the party who first provided the "Contract".
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Contracts' as lenders, agents, brokers, clients, or others. The changed text replaces 'Contracts' with 'Deals', which means that the contradicted text, which refers to exclusive and valuable 'Contracts', now contradicts the changed text, because 'Contracts' is no longer the term used to refer to lenders, agents, brokers, etc. This creates a contradiction because the definition of what is considered a 'Contract' has been altered, causing a misalignment of terminology and conflicting obligations as per the Perturbation Definitions. The modified text creates a clear contradiction within the document.

perturbation.original_text: Essentially, the spirit behind this Agreement is one of mutual trust, confidence and reliance upon each party to do what is fair and equitable.
perturbation.changed_text: Essentially, the spirit behind this Agreement is one of mutual trust, confidence and reliance upon each entity to do what is fair and equitable.
perturbation.explanation: The term "Party" is changed to "Entity". Since there was already a change of party to entity, the document will become contradictory in which of those terms are referring to the original signing parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties agree to abide by the following rules of non-circumvention and non-disclosure for a period of Two years from the effective date hereof. Such covenant and agreement shall survive termination of this Agreement for any reason whatsoever. a) Each Party, for itself and its associates as defined below, represents and warrants that it shall not conduct business with any sources or contacts, or said source's or contact's associates as defined below, that are originally made known and/or available by another Party hereto, at any time or in any manner, without the express written permission (not to be unreasonably withheld) of the Party who made the source(s) known and/or available. b) For purposes of this Agreement, the term "associates" or "contacts" shall be defined as: in the case of a business entity its officers, directors, affiliates, subsidiaries, associated entities, and any other business entity in which the business entity owns five percent (5%) or more of the outstanding equity interest. c) The Parties will maintain complete confidentiality regarding this Agreement and all transactions occurring thereunder, each other's business, business sources and affiliates and each other's propriety knowledge and know-how, and will disclose such information only pursuant to the express written permission of the party who made such information available save where such information deemed to be in the public domain or under the order of a competent Court or Government Agency. d) This Agreement and each additional agreement concluded or written or verbal disclosure made between the Parties, shall be kept confidential and is not to be reproduced, communicated or distributed in any manner whatsoever except on a "need to know" basis to persons directly involved with the closing of any transaction contemplated between the Parties, or legal counsel of a Party. e) It is understood and agreed that by reason of this "Agreement" the "Parties" that are involved during the course of business transactions may learn from one another, or from the principals the names, addresses, telephone numbers <PAGE> of lenders, agents, brokers, clients or others hereafter referred to as "Contracts" and or "Associates". f) It is understood and agreed that the "Contracts" of each party hereto are and shall be recognized as exclusive and valuable "Contracts" and that the parties will not directly or indirectly negotiate or participate in any transaction circumventing the party who first provided the "Contract".
perturbation.justification: The change from "Party" to "Entity" creates a contradiction because the contradicted text repeatedly refers to "Parties" and their specific obligations, such as non-circumvention and non-disclosure. By changing "Party" to "Entity" in the changed text, it introduces ambiguity about who is bound by those obligations and makes enforcement unclear since the document now uses two different terms for what was likely intended to be the same concept. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology definition, which states that using multiple terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations.
